diagrams.title: Independent Variables in Unit Square
diagrams.description: Joint density of two independent Uniform(0,1) variables showing the rectangular region where probability concentrates
diagrams.purpose: Visualize how independence creates factorable joint distribution - the joint density is simply the product of marginal densities, forming a rectangular prism in 3D.

diagrams.title: Convolution for Sum of Independent Dice
diagrams.description: Grid showing all possible sums when rolling two independent dice
diagrams.purpose: Understand convolution visually: P(X+Y=k) is the sum along the k-th diagonal. This triangular pattern emerges from the convolution of two discrete uniform distributions.

diagrams.title: Covariance and Correlation Visualization
diagrams.description: Scatter plots showing different relationships between variables
elements: Elliptical elongated cloud for positively correlated
Diamond-shaped cloud for uncorrelated but dependent (Y=X²)
Negative correlation shows diagonal going down
diagrams.purpose: Distinguish between independence, correlation, and dependence. Independent variables show no pattern; uncorrelated can still show clear patterns (non-linear dependence).
